sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check if clicking on top-left grid changed anything
print("Board changed:", last_action_result.get('board_changed', False))
print("Level completed:", last_action_result.get('level_completed', False))

# Let me check the top-left grid to see if anything changed
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nTop-left grid (row 12, col 20 area):")
for r in range(10, 18):
    print(f"  {r}: '{lines[r][18:24]}'")

# Also check bottom-right to make sure nothing changed there
print("\nBottom-right outer grid:")
for r in [38, 46, 54]:
    row_str = lines[r]
    c0 = row_str[36:42]
    c1 = row_str[44:50]
    c2 = row_str[52:58]
    print(f"  Row {r}: {c0} {c1} {c2}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK so clicking on the top-left grid didn't change anything. The top-left grid cells are not interactive. Only the bottom-right grid cells are clickable.
Now let me reconsider the problem. The bottom-right grid currently is:
R b b
R . R
b b R
This matches the bottom-left exactly. But the level isn't complete. I need to figure out what the correct transformation is.
Let me go back to the top example and look at it more carefully:
Top-left:
b R b
R . b
b R R
Top-right:
b R b
R . R
b R b
The changes are:
- (1,2): b -> R
- (2,2): R -> b
This makes each row a palindrome (mirror left to right).
Now for bottom-left:
R b b
R . R
b b R
Applying the same "mirror left to right" rule:
- Row 0: [R, b, b] -> [R, b, R] (copy R to position 2)
- Row 1: [R, ., R] -> [R, ., R] (already symmetric)
- Row 2: [b, b, R] -> [b, b, b] (copy b to position 2)
Target: R b R / R . R / b b b
I already tried this and it didn't complete the level. But wait - maybe I need to also consider the center cell transformation.
Let me look at the center cell transformation again:
Top-left center:
g W g
W R g
g W W
Top-right center:
g W g
W R W
g W g
Changes:
- (1,2): g -> W
- (2,2): W -> g
Applying "mirror left to right":
- Row 0: [g, W, g] -> [g, W, g] (already symmetric)
- Row 1: [W, R, g] -> [W, R, W] (copy W to position 2)
- Row 2: [g, W, W] -> [g, W, g] (copy g to position 2)
This matches! So the rule is "mirror left to right" for both the outer grid AND the center cell.
Now for the bottom-left center:
W g g
W R W
g g W
Applying "mirror left to right":
- Row 0: [W, g, g] -> [W, g, W] (copy W to position 2)
- Row 1: [W, R, W] -> [W, R, W] (already symmetric)
- Row 2: [g, g, W] -> [g, g, g] (copy g to position 2)
Target center: W g W / W R W / g g g
But I can't click on the center cell! So maybe the center cell is automatically updated when the outer grid is correct?
Wait, but I already set the outer grid to the symmetric version (R b R / R . R / b b b) and the level wasn't complete. Unless... the center cell DID change but I didn't notice?
Let me check the current state of the bottom-right center cell. Currently the bottom-right outer grid is R b b / R . R / b b R (matching bottom-left). Let me check the center.
Actually, I think I need to set the outer grid to the symmetric version AND the center cell should automatically update. But when I tried the symmetric version before, the level wasn't complete. Maybe the center cell didn't update automatically, and I need to find another way to change it.
Or maybe the center cell changes are triggered by clicking on the outer grid cells in a specific order or pattern?
Let me try setting the outer grid to the symmetric version again and carefully checking the center cell afterward.

sections.content:
World model: Top-left grid cells are NOT clickable - only bottom-right cells are interactive. The transformation rule is "mirror left to right" for both outer grid and center cell. I need to set the outer grid to the symmetric version and check if the center cell updates.
Plan: Set the outer grid to the symmetric version (R b R / R . R / b b b) and carefully check the center cell after.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current bottom-right: R b b / R . R / b b R
# Target symmetric: R b R / R . R / b b b
# Need to toggle (0,2): b -> R and (2,2): R -> b

# First, let me check the current center cell state
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("Current bottom-right center cell:")
for r in range(44, 50):
    print(f"  {r}: '{lines[r][44:50]}'")

# Now set the outer grid to symmetric
predict(expect_change=True, note='Setting outer grid to symmetric version')
action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 38, 'col': 54},  # (0,2): b -> R
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 54, 'col': 54}     # (2,2): R -> b
])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
